title: Practice Problem Generator
text:
Hypothesis Testing with z-scores
Given the following data, is the mean of \(Group_A\) significantly different from the population mean: \(\mu = {11}\)? Use a \({2}\) tailed-test with \(\alpha = {0.01}\)





A




9


20


13


12


15


8


12


21


16


15


15


12


11


8



The necessary summary statistics for these data \[M_A = {13.36}\] \[{\sigma} = {3}\] \[n = {14}\]
State the Hypotheses  \[H_0: \mu = 11\] \[H_1: \mu \ne 11\] 
The decision criteria:  \(z_{crit} = \pm{2.58}, \alpha_{two-tailed} = {0.01}\) Calculate the standard error  \[\sigma_M = \frac{\sigma}{\sqrt{N}}\]  \[\sigma_M = \frac{3}{\sqrt{14}}\]  \[\sigma_M = \frac{3}{3.74}\]  \[\sigma_M = {0.8}\]  Calculate \(z_{obt}\)  \[z_{obt} = {\frac{M - \mu}{\sigma_M}}\]  \[z_{obt} = \frac{13.36 - 11}{0.8}\]  \[z_{obt} = \frac{2.36}{0.8}\]  \[z_{obt} = {2.95}\]  Calculate Cohen’s d for effect size  \[d = \frac{M - \mu}{\sigma}\]  \[d = \frac{13.36 - 11}{3}\]  \[d = \frac{2.36}{3}\]  \[d = {0.79}\]  The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  z = 2.95, p < 0.01, d = 0.79

title: Practice Problem Generator
text:
One-Sample t-test
Given the following data, is the mean of \(Group_A\) significantly different from \({44}\)? Use a \({2}\) tailed-test with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) 





A




47


71


43


61


50


52



 The necessary summary statistics for these data \[M_A = {54.0}\] \[s^2 = {105.6}\] \[n = {6}\]
State the Hypotheses  \[H_0: \mu = 44\] \[H_1: \mu \ne 44\] 
The decision criteria:  \(t_{crit} = \pm{2.57}, \alpha_{two-tailed} = {0.05}, df = {5}\) Calculate the standard error  \[s_M = \sqrt{\frac{s^2}{n}}\]  \[s_M = \sqrt{\frac{105.6}{6}}\]  \[s_M = \sqrt{17.6}\]  \[s_M = {4.2}\]  calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{M - \mu}{s_M}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{54.0 - 44}{4.2}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{10.0}{4.2}\]  \[t_{obt} = {2.38}\]  calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M - \mu}{s}\]  \[d = \frac{54.0 - 44}{10.28}\]  \[d = \frac{10.0}{10.28}\]  \[d = {0.97}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  t(5) = 2.38, p > 0.05, d = 0.97

title: Practice Problem Generator
text:
Independent-Samples t-test
Given the following between-subjects data, is the mean of \(Group_A\) significantly greater than the mean of \(Group_B\)? Use a \({1}\) tailed-test with \(\alpha = {0.05}\)





A
B




50
46


49
62


57
61


49
51


40
52


52
48


53
52


63
43


51

55
62


52
55


62
60


50
57



 The necessary summary Statistis for these data: \[M_A = {52.54}, M_B = {54.0}\] \[SS_A = {423.23}, SS_B = {462.0}\] \[n_A = {13}, n_B = {13}\]
State the Hypotheses  \[H_0: \mu_A - \mu_B \leq 0\] \[H_1: \mu_A - \mu_B \gt 0\] 
The decision criteria:  \(t_{crit} = +{1.71}, \alpha_{one-tailed} = {0.05}, df = {24}\) Calculate the pooled variance:  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{SS_A + SS_B}{df_A + df_B}}\]  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{423.23 + 462.0}{12 + 12}}\]  \[s_p^2 = \frac{885.23}{24}\]  \[s_p^2 = {36.88}\] 
Calculate the estimated error of the difference between means: \(s_{(M_A - M_B)}\)  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{s_p^2}{n_1} + \frac{s_p^2}{n_1}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{36.88}{13} + \frac{36.88}{13}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{2.84 + 2.84}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{5.68}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = {2.38}\] 
Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{(M_A - M_B) - (\mu_A - \mu_B)}{s_{(M_A - M_B)}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{(52.54 - 54.0) - {0}}{2.38}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{-1.46}{2.38}\]  \[t_{obt} = {-0.61}\] 
Calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_A - M_B}{\sqrt{s_p^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{(52.54 - 54.0)}{{{\sqrt{36.88}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{(-1.46)}{6.07}\]  \[d = {-0.24}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  t(24) = -0.61, p > 0.05, d = -0.24

The decision criteria:  \(t_{crit} = {-2.13}, \alpha_{one-tailed} = {0.05}, df = {4}\) Calculating the difference scores \(D = X_B - X_A\)

Calculate the Mean of the Difference Scores \[M_D = \frac{\Sigma D}{n}\]  \[M_D = \frac{106}{5}\]  \[M_D = {21.2}\]

New Summary statistics for the difference scores: \[M_D = {21.2}, \quad \Sigma{D} = {106}, \quad \Sigma{D^2} = {2470}\] 
Calculate SS of the difference scores  \[SS_D = \Sigma D^2 - \frac{(\Sigma D)^2}{n}\]  \[SS_D = {2470} - \frac{11236}{5}\]  \[SS_D = {2470} - {2247.2}\]  \[SS_D = {222.8}\] 
Calculate the variance of the difference scores  \[s_D^2 = \frac{SS_D}{df}\]  \[s_D^2 = \frac{222.8}{4}\]  \[s_D^2 = {55.7}\] 
Calculate the estimated standard error of the difference scores  \[s_{M_D} = \sqrt{\frac{s^2}{n}}\]  \[s_{M_D} = \sqrt{\frac{55.7}{5}}\]  \[s_{M_D} = \sqrt{11.14}\]  \[s_{M_D} = {3.34}\] 
Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{M_D - \mu_D}{s_{M_D}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{21.2 - 0}{3.34}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{21.2}{3.34}\]  \[t_{obt} = {6.35}\] 
Calculating Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_D}{\sqrt{s^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{21.2}{{{\sqrt{55.7}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{21.2}{7.46}\]  \[d = {2.84}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  t(4) = 6.35, p > 0.05, d = 2.84

Summary statistics for these data: \(G = {3549} \quad \Sigma{X^2} = {214593} \quad k = {4} \quad N = {60}\) 
\(T_{A} = {845} \quad SS_{A} = {1125.33}\)  \(T_{B} = {825} \quad SS_{B} = {1004.0}\)  \(T_{C} = {878} \quad SS_{C} = {779.73}\)  \(T_{D} = {1001} \quad SS_{D} = {514.93}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.15}, \alpha = {0.01}\) 
Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {60} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {59}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {4} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {3}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {60} - {4}\]  \[df_{within} = {56}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {214593} - \frac{3549^2}{60}\]  \[SS_{total} = {214593} - \frac{12595401}{60}\]  \[SS_{total} = {214593} - {209923.35}\]  \[SS_{total} = {4669.65}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {1125.33 + 1004.0 + 779.73 + 514.93}\]  \[SS_{within} = {3423.99}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {4669.65} - {3423.99}\]  \[SS_{between} = {1245.66}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{1245.66}{3}\]  \[MS_{between} = {415.22}\] 
\[MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}\]  \[MS_{within} = \frac{3423.99}{56}\]  \[MS_{within} = {61.14}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{415.22}{61.14}\]  \[F_{obt} = {6.79}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{1245.66}{4669.65}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.27}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({3}, {56}) = {6.79}, p < {0.01}, \eta^2 = {0.27}\)

Summary statistics for these data: \(G = {1026} \quad \Sigma X^2 = {20158} \quad k = {5} \quad N = {55}\) 
\(T_{A} = {207} \quad SS_{A} = {219.64}\)  \(T_{B} = {229} \quad SS_{B} = {173.64}\)  \(T_{C} = {168} \quad SS_{C} = {150.18}\)  \(T_{D} = {188} \quad SS_{D} = {100.91}\)  \(T_{E} = {234} \quad SS_{E} = {94.18}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D = \mu_E\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {2.61}, \alpha = {0.05}\) 
Stage 1 Calculations:  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {55} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {54}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {5} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {4}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {55} - {5}\]  \[df_{within} = {50}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {20158} - \frac{1026^2}{55}\]  \[SS_{total} = {20158} - \frac{1052676}{55}\]  \[SS_{total} = {20158} - {19139.56}\]  \[SS_{total} = {1018.44}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {219.64 + 173.64 + 150.18 + 100.91 + 94.18}\]  \[SS_{within} = {738.55}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {1018.44} - {738.55}\]  \[SS_{between} = {279.89}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Stage 2 Calculations:  Partition the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{subjects} = n - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {11} - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {10}\]  \[df_{error} = df_{within} - df_{subjects}\]  \[df_{error} = {50} - {10}\]  \[df_{error} = {40}\]

\[SS_{subjects} = \Sigma{\frac{P^2}{k}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {\frac{95^2}{5} + \frac{97^2}{5} + \frac{87^2}{5} + \frac{101^2}{5} + \frac{84^2}{5} + \frac{98^2}{5} + \frac{103^2}{5} + \frac{75^2}{5} + \frac{97^2}{5} + \frac{97^2}{5} + \frac{92^2}{5}} - \frac{1026^2}{55}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {\frac{9025}{5} + \frac{9409}{5} + \frac{7569}{5} + \frac{10201}{5} + \frac{7056}{5} + \frac{9604}{5} + \frac{10609}{5} + \frac{5625}{5} + \frac{9409}{5} + \frac{9409}{5} + \frac{8464}{5}} - \frac{1052676}{55}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {{1805.0} + {1881.8} + {1513.8} + {2040.2} + {1411.2} + {1920.8} + {2121.8} + {1125.0} + {1881.8} + {1881.8} + {1692.8}} - {19139.56}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {19276.0} - {19139.56}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {136.44}\]  \[SS_{error} = SS_{within} - SS_{subjects}\]  \[SS_{error} = {738.55} - {136.44}\]  \[SS_{error} = {602.11}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{279.89}{4}\]  \[MS_{between} = {69.97}\] 
\[MS_{error} = \frac{SS_{error}}{df_{error}}\]  \[MS_{error} = \frac{602.11}{40}\]  \[MS_{error} = {15.05}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{error}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{69.97}{15.05}\]  \[F_{obt} = {4.65}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total} - SS_{subjects}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{279.89}{{1018.44} - {136.44}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{279.89}{882.0}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = {0.32}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {40}) = {4.65}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.32}\)

title: Practice Problem Generator
text:
Two-Factor ANOVA
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {330} \quad M_{A1B1} = {33.0}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {132.0} \quad n_{A1B1} = {10}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {295} \quad M_{A1B2} = {29.5}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {98.5} \quad n_{A1B2} = {10}\)  \(T_{A1B3} = {281} \quad M_{A1B3} = {28.1}\quad SS_{A1B3} = {140.9} \quad n_{A1B3} = {10}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {352} \quad M_{A2B1} = {35.2}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {169.6} \quad n_{A2B1} = {10}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {278} \quad M_{A2B2} = {27.8}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {225.6} \quad n_{A2B2} = {10}\)  \(T_{A2B3} = {393} \quad M_{A2B3} = {39.3}\quad SS_{A2B3} = {312.1} \quad n_{A2B3} = {10}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {60} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {59}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {6} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {5}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {60} - {6}\)  \(df_{within} = {54}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {64131} - \frac{1929^2}{60}\)  \(SS_{total} = {64131} - \frac{3721041}{60}\)  \(SS_{total} = {64131} - {62017.35}\)  \(SS_{total} = {2113.65}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {132.0 + 98.5 + 140.9 + 169.6 + 225.6 + 312.1}\)  \(SS_{within} = {1078.7}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {2113.65} - {1078.7}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1034.95}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {906}  \quad n_{A_1} = {30}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {1023}  \quad n_{A_2} = {30}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.13}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{906}^2}{30} + \frac{{1023}^2}{30}} - \frac{{1929}^2}{60}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{820836}}{30} + \frac{{1046529}}{30}} - \frac{{3721041}}{60}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{27361.2} + {34884.3}} - {62017.35}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {62245.5} - {62017.35}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {228.15}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{228.15}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {228.15}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{228.15}{19.98}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {11.42}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{228.15}{{2113.65} - {369.1} - {437.7}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{228.15}{1306.85}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.17}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {54}) = {11.42}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.17}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {682}  \quad n_{B_1} = {20}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {573}  \quad n_{B_2} = {20}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {674}  \quad n_{B_3} = {20}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {5.02}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{682}^2}{20} + \frac{{573}^2}{20} + \frac{{674}^2}{20}} - \frac{{1929}^2}{60}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{465124}}{20} + \frac{{328329}}{20} + \frac{{454276}}{20}} - \frac{{3721041}}{60}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{23256.2} + {16416.45} + {22713.8}} - {62017.35}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {62386.45} - {62017.35}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {369.1}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{369.1}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {184.55}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{184.55}{19.98}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {9.24}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{369.1}{{2113.65} - {228.15} - {437.7}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{369.1}{1447.8}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.25}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {54}) = {9.24}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.25}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {1} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {5.02}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1034.95} - {228.15} - {369.1}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {437.7}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{437.7}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {218.85}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{218.85}{19.98}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {10.95}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{437.7}{{2113.65} - {228.15} - {369.1}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{437.7}{1516.4}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.29}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {54}) = {10.95}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.29}\)
